lesson.title: Extrema & Critical Points
lesson.body: A critical point of f is where f'(c) = 0 or f'(c) is undefined. The First Derivative Test: if f' changes from + to − at c, then c is a local maximum; if − to +, a local minimum. The Second Derivative Test: if f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0, then c is a local minimum; if f''(c) < 0, a local maximum. Absolute extrema on [a,b] occur at critical points or endpoints.

rooms.question: A critical point of f is a value c where:
answers: f'(c) = 0 or f'(c) does not exist
rooms.explanation: Critical points include where the derivative equals zero (horizontal tangent) or is undefined.

rooms.question: Find the critical points of f(x) = x³ − 3x.
For f(x) = −x² + 4x, the second derivative test at x = 2 gives:
rooms.explanation: f''(2) = −2 < 0, so f is concave down at x=2, confirming a local maximum.

rooms.question: Order the closed interval method to find absolute extrema on [a,b]:
items: Find all critical points of f in (a,b)
Evaluate f at each critical point
Evaluate f at the endpoints a and b
Compare all values: largest is max, smallest is min
rooms.explanation: Absolute extrema on a closed interval occur either at critical points or endpoints — evaluate all candidates and compare.

rooms.question: If f'(x) > 0 on (a, b), then f is:
answers: Has a maximum at some c in (a, b)
rooms.explanation: Positive derivative ↔ increasing function. Negative derivative ↔ decreasing.

boss.name: The Critical Point
boss.taunt: At my peak, the slope is zero. But am I maximum or minimum? Prove it.

questions.question: f(x) = x⁴ has a critical point at x = 0 with f''(0) = 0. This means the second derivative test is:
answers: Inconclusive — check the first derivative test instead
questions.explanation: When f''(c) = 0, the second derivative test is inconclusive. Use the first derivative test: f'(x) = 4x³ changes from − to + at 0, confirming a minimum.

questions.question: The absolute maximum of f(x) = x³ − 3x on [0, 2] is:
questions.explanation: Critical points: f'=3x²−3=0 → x=1 (in [0,2]). Values: f(0)=0, f(1)=−2, f(2)=2. Absolute max is 2 at x=2.

questions.question: An inflection point of f occurs where:
questions.explanation: An inflection point is where f'' changes sign (concavity switches). f''=0 is necessary but not sufficient — f'' must change sign.
